title: trollius kansuensis
intro: Trollius kansuensis, also known as the Tibetan globeflower, is a species of flowering plant in the buttercup family that is native to the high-altitude regions of the Tibetan Plateau. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and anti-bacterial properties, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The Trollius kansuensis is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and treating respiratory problems due to its expectorant properties. It can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and in some regions, it is also used as a topical application for skin issues and wounds.
